channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Tue, 14 Jul 2026 11:33:31 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Meet Anil Menon: Indian-origin Nasa astronaut set to liftoff on first 8-month ISS mission today; here’s what he will do in space
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Turkey's Cappadocia region was home to free-roaming horses, then local cowboys began gathering them for guided photo tours, turning the iconic herds into a tourism attraction that's helping preserve them
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Meet the magnetic liquid metal robot: It merges and squeezes through tiny gaps like a living cell
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Japan's 2011 megaquake sent a seismic wave nearly 2,900 km to Earth's core, which returned 13 minutes later and shifted the entire country eastward
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Alec Baldwin's wife Hilaria Baldwin: "With breath on your side, you become alert and aware of what's really going on, not overtaken by…" - why one conscious breath can help you think more clearly
item.description: Hilaria Baldwin suggests using breath for greater awareness and clarity. Conscious breathing creates a pause, allowing for observation instead of instinct. This pause helps overcome ego and old destructive patterns effectively. Modern research supports slower breathing for stress reduction and relaxation. This simple technique aids in making better decisions in daily life.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Why did Tom Segura and Christina P separate after their 18-year marriage? Podcasters’ split details emerge
item.description: Tom Segura and Christina P have reportedly separated after 18 years of marriage, according to TMZ. The news has surprised many fans who have followed the couple’s journey through comedy, podcasts and television projects. While the pair have remained largely private about the situation, reports suggest they will continue working together, leaving plenty of questions still unanswered.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: 9 European nations, Ukraine form an anti-ballistic missile coalition
item.description: Nine European countries and Ukraine established a coalition for defensive anti-ballistic capabilities. This alliance aims to deter and defeat future missile threats effectively. The group will integrate defense industries, research, and operational experiences. Ukrainian President Zelensky attended a Paris meeting to reaffirm support for his nation. This initiative responds to growing ballistic missile threats and Ukraine's unique experiences.

item.title: LeT, IS operatives among 34 killed in Khuzdar attack: BLA
item.description: The Baloch Liberation Army claimed a major suicide attack on an ISIS-K commander's compound. This operation targeted a meeting coordinating efforts against the Baloch political movement. The BLA stated that 34 operatives, including ISIS and LeT members, were killed. The attack, executed by the Majeed Brigade, followed weeks of intelligence surveillance. This incident highlights alleged links between Pakistan's ISI and militant groups.
item.dc:creator: Raj Shekhar Jha

item.title: “He was punching me”: Ria Jalesa accuses Beyoncé’s nephew Julez Smith of hotel assault, says she was left with bruises
item.description: Twitch streamer Ria Jalesa has accused Julez Smith, Beyoncé’s nephew and Solange Knowles’ son, of physically assaulting her during a hotel encounter. She claims he left her with bruises, damaged her phone, pointed a firearm at her, and threatened her life. Ria shared her allegations in livestreams, while Smith has not publicly responded so far.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: UK: Peterborough council denies sale of Hindu temple to mosque is ‘unlawful’
item.description: Peterborough city council argued its decision to sell a complex housing a Hindu temple was not unlawful. The council stated that while Hindu faith is protected, it does not dictate decisions. The Hindu community expressed disbelief at the council's valuation of alternative premises. The council needs the money from the sale to the UK Islamic Mission. Bharat Hindu Samaj is seeking a judicial review to quash the council's decision.
item.dc:creator: Naomi Canton

item.title: Clavicular claims Israel Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's advisers suggested a gym livestream to reach younger voters
item.description: A streamer named Clavicular claims Netanyahu's advisers sought his help. They reportedly wanted a gym livestream to improve the leader's image. Clavicular stated advisers believed Netanyahu appeared old and out of shape. This claim remains unverified by any official confirmation. A meeting with an adviser was confirmed at a nightclub.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Quote of the day by Justin Trudeau: 'I don’t read the newspapers, I don’t watch the news. I figure, if something important happens, someone will tell me' and his detachment from politics
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Colombian man with Social Security Number, work authorization killed by ICE agents in Maine: 'He was bleeding profusely from the head'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Marcus Aurelius: 'The only wealth which you will keep forever is the wealth...'
item.description: The philosophical musings of ancient Roman thinkers reveal that genuine wealth stems from the act of giving. Unlike material possessions, which fade over time, generosity fosters enduring character and deep social connections. This age-old wisdom stands in stark contrast to the practice of hoarding, which provides merely an illusion of security. Today, embracing generosity mirrors contemporary charitable endeavors and strategic investments, ultimately converting fleeting assets into lasting positive impact.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Dale Carnegie: "Don't be afraid of enemies who attack you. Be afraid of…" - why honest criticism is more valuable than praise
item.description: Dale Carnegie cautioned against trusting flattering friends more than open enemies. He argued that sincere criticism offers valuable lessons for personal growth. Flattery creates a false sense of security, hindering honest self-reflection. As influence grows, people often avoid challenging powerful individuals. Seeking truthful feedback remains crucial for lasting judgment and improvement.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the Day by Napoleon Bonaparte: 'Never interrupt your enemy when he is making a mistake'
item.description: The concept of strategic patience can provide a significant edge in competitive environments. By letting rivals make their own missteps, one can capitalize on the ensuing turmoil. Historical figures like Napoleon Bonaparte utilized this method effectively at the Battle of Austerlitz. In contemporary business and political arenas, this approach highlights the importance of timing and self-restraint rather than hasty actions.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Kai Cenat drops ‘EBK’ diss track targeting FunnyMike, DDG, Rakai and other Streamer University critics
item.description: Kai Cenat has released a diss track titled EBK, targeting creators who criticized his event. The song addresses complaints about the Streamer University 2026 selection process. FunnyMike and DDG were specifically called out in the new track. This online feud continues as the event approaches its scheduled start. Fans anticipate further responses from involved creators and critics.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Early humans fed salmon to canines 12,000 years ago, that is why dogs are friendly companions
item.description: Recent findings reveal that ancient dogs in Alaska had a unique partnership with humans, dating back 12,000 years. This evidence indicates that these canines often shared salmon with early settlers, shedding light on the evolving dynamics between humans and dogs in the Americas. The analysis of fossilized bones uncovered a diet rich in fish, prompting a reevaluation of wolf domestication theories and illustrating a nuanced history of human-canine interactions.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by former Japanese Prime Minister Shinzo Abe: "My opinion is that politicians should be humble in the face of history. And whenever history is a matter of debate, it…" - a thoughtful reflection reminds us that history deserves humility, evidence and careful scholarship, not political convenience
item.description: Shinzo Abe believed politicians should be humble regarding history. He stated that historians and experts should handle debated historical issues. Abe's own record on wartime conduct faced criticism from historians. This highlights a gap between his stated principle and actions. His words remind us that history requires careful scholarship, not political convenience.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Rishi Sunak: 'The greatest sacrifice I have made is that I have been an appalling husband and father for the past couple of years'
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
